node.title: Uber | Phone Screen | Find all non-overlapping intervals

topic.title: Uber | Phone Screen | Find all non-overlapping intervals

post.content:
You are given a set of non-overlapping `intervals` and a `requestInterval`. Find all non-overlapping intervals.

**Example 1:**
```
Input: intervals = [[10,15], [25,35]], requestInterval = [17,27]
Output: [[17,25]]
```

**Example 2:**
```
Input: intervals = [[10,15], [25,35], [45,65], [85,95]], requestInterval = [17,100]
Output: [[17,25], [35,45], [65,85], [95,100]]
```

#working solution in python 3 
class Interval:
     def __init__(self, s=0, e=0):
         self.start = s
         self.end = e

def remaining(intervals, newI):
    res=[]
    res.append(newI)
    for i in range(len(intervals)):
        sz = len(res)
        nI = res[sz-1]
        topI = intervals[i]
        if (topI.start <= nI.start <= topI.end) and (topI.start <= nI.end <= topI.end):
            return []
        if (topI.start <= nI.start <= topI.end):
            res.pop()
            res.append(Interval(topI.end,nI.end))
        elif (topI.start <= nI.end <= topI.end):
            res.pop()
            res.append(Interval(nI.start,topI.start))
        elif (nI.start< topI.start) and (nI.end > topI.end):
            res.pop()
            res.append(Interval(nI.start,topI.start))
            res.append(Interval(topI.end,nI.end))
    return res
    
def main():
    arrIv = []
    iv = Interval(10,15)
    arrIv.append(iv)
    iv = Interval(25,35)
    arrIv.append(iv)
    iv = Interval(45,65)
    arrIv.append(iv)
    iv = Interval(85,95)
    arrIv.append(iv)
    nI = Interval(17,100)
    res = remaining(arrIv, nI)
    print ("anser sz", len(res))
    for ele in res:
        print(ele.start,ele.end)
        
if __name__=="__main__":
    main()
